title: Session dashboard and detail pages
description:
SessionDashboard with live session cards, burn rate sparkline. SessionDetail with metadata and subagent list. Real-time via useSSEStream.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-08-ui-architecture.md section 8.3

title: Session tracker and aggregator
description:
Implement SessionTracker (goroutine-safe map), ActiveSession state, Aggregator poll loop (2s). Read from 3 sources: SQLite api_requests, SQLite events, JSONL files. Burn rate calculation using internal/metrics/.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-07-daemon-monitoring.md sections 4.1-4.4
Shared dep: internal/metrics/ (BurnRate from Wave 0), internal/pricing/ (cost calc from Wave 0)

NOTE: All proxy logs and hook events are PERMANENT in SQLite. The daemon's in-memory ActiveSession state is a derived working set rebuilt from permanent data on restart. Nothing unique lives only in memory except real-time burn rate samples.

Acceptance criteria:
- New session appears within 4 seconds of first API request
- Session marked inactive after 5 minutes idle
- Burn rate non-zero when session is active
- Subagent spawns/stops reflected in ActiveSubagents

Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md

title: session-lens decision documentation
description:
Document that session-lens is superseded by SPEC-04 for structural analysis. Add ht sessions summarize to backlog if chosen.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-10-integrations.md section 5

title: Minimal Chrome app shell
description:
Create frontend/ directory with Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4. Add cc-web-ui as local file dep. Implement launchUI() in Go with WSL detection. Serve embedded dist/ from daemon. Add web manifest.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-08-ui-architecture.md sections 6.1, 7.1-7.2, 8.1
Cross-repo: ~/dev/cc-web-ui (local file dependency)

Acceptance criteria:
- ht ui opens Chrome app window
- Window title shows 'Hitch'
- WSL detection works
- Fallback to default browser if no Chrome

title: Wave 2A: SPEC-03 Request Body Inspection
description:
Enhanced request body parsing, token attribution, ht proxy inspect breakdown, ht proxy why command, ht proxy gaps command.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-03-request-inspection.md
Existing code: internal/proxy/analyze.go (has RequestAnalysis types — must reconcile, not duplicate)
Shared deps: internal/pricing/, internal/tokens/ (from Wave 0)

Blocks: Wave 4 (SPEC-07 daemon last-request view)

title: RequestBreakdown types and parser
description:
Implement ParseRequestFile, RequestBreakdown, ComponentBreakdown, MessageBreakdown, FileReadInfo, ToolCallInfo types. Extend existing analyze.go — reconcile with existing RequestAnalysis types (extend, don't duplicate). Implement classifySystemBlock, buildToolUseIndex, extractToolResultText, estimateTokens (use internal/tokens/ package), computePercentages, topN.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-03-request-inspection.md sections 2-5
Existing: internal/proxy/analyze.go (has RequestAnalysis — READ FIRST, reconcile types)
Shared dep: internal/tokens/ (from Wave 0)

Test each classification rule, edge cases (string system field, string content, corrupt JSON, very large tool results).
Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md
notes:
## Completion
All criteria MET. RequestBreakdown types and ParseRequestFile implemented. System block classification, tool extraction, token estimation via tokens.Estimate(). 37 new tests.
Files: internal/proxy/analyze.go, analyze_test.go

title: Enhanced ht proxy inspect with breakdown
description:
Modify runProxyInspect to call ParseRequestFile and render the breakdown. Add printBreakdown, formatTokenCount, truncateLabel helpers. Show breakdown when request log file exists, graceful degradation when it doesn't.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-03-request-inspection.md section 6.1
Existing: internal/cli/proxy.go runProxyInspect function

Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md
notes: All criteria MET. printBreakdown, formatTokenCount, truncateLabel added to proxy inspect.

title: ht proxy why command
description:
New CLI subcommand. Generates natural-language one-paragraph explanation from RequestBreakdown. Template-based sentence construction per spec.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-03-request-inspection.md sections 6.3, 7.1 (GenerateWhySummary)
Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md
notes: All criteria MET. newProxyWhyCmd registered, calls ParseRequestFile + GenerateWhySummary.

title: ht proxy gaps command
description:
New CLI subcommand. Correlates missing proxy log entries with StopFailure hook events to detect false rate limiter incidents. Queries hitch.db for StopFailure events and api_requests within time windows.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-03-request-inspection.md section 6.4
Existing: internal/state/proxy.go, internal/state/events.go
Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md
notes: All criteria MET. newProxyGapsCmd with QueryStopFailureEvents, QueryRequestsNear. 5 tests.

title: Wave 2A test quality review
description: Fresh-context review of Wave 2A tests. Spec section 9 has full test list: TestClassifySystemBlock, TestEstimateTokens, TestBuildToolUseIndex, TestExtractToolResultText, TestParseMessageBody, TestGenerateWhySummary, TestProxyGaps.

title: Wave 2A docs + tryout creator
description:
Create internal/proxy/README.md update (add inspect breakdown, why, gaps commands). Update CLAUDE.md proxy section. Then create tryout task in hitch-r3u with instructions to:
1. ./ht proxy inspect <recent-id> (verify breakdown appears)
2. ./ht proxy why <recent-id>
3. ./ht proxy gaps --since 24h
4. Test with various request sizes

title: Wave 3A: SPEC-05 Settings TUI
description:
Interactive bubbletea TUI with 5 tabs: Settings, Env Vars, Hooks, Memory & Context, Explorer. Launched via ht settings.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-05-settings-tui.md
Deps: pkg/settings and pkg/envvars from Wave 1A
Reference tables: private/reference/tables-settings.md, private/reference/tables-env-vars.md, private/reference/tables-hooks.md
New deps: github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea, bubbles, lipgloss (added in Wave 0)

title: Bubbletea top-level model and tab navigation
description:
Implement the top-level Model struct, tab switching (Tab/Shift+Tab, 1-5 keys), help overlay, status bar, resize handling, and the tabModel interface. Create internal/tui/ package structure.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-05-settings-tui.md sections 3, 4 (top-level model, messages, keys)
New package: internal/tui/
Deps: github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea, bubbles, lipgloss (added in Wave 0)

Acceptance criteria:
- ht settings launches TUI, shows tab bar, q exits cleanly
- Tab/Shift+Tab cycles tabs, 1-5 jumps to tab
- ? toggles help overlay
- Terminal resize handled (tea.WindowSizeMsg)
- go build ./... compiles

Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md

title: Settings tab (Tab 1)
description:
Implement SettingsModel: grouped key display with effective values and scope badges, inline editor, filter. Uses pkg/settings.LoadAll() and Schema().

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-05-settings-tui.md section 2 (Tab 1), section 4 (SettingsModel state)
Deps: pkg/settings from Wave 1A
Reference: private/reference/tables-settings.md

Acceptance criteria:
- All 56+ settings keys shown grouped by category
- Effective value and scope badge (color-coded) displayed per key
- Managed-only keys show read-only, no editor
- Editing saves to correct scope and refreshes display
- / filter narrows visible rows

Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md

title: Env Vars tab (Tab 2)
description:
Implement EnvVarsModel: 128 vars grouped by category, quick toggles, source badges, sensitive value masking, edit/remove from env block.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-05-settings-tui.md section 2 (Tab 2)
Deps: pkg/envvars from Wave 1A
Reference: private/reference/tables-env-vars.md

Follow the UI Bubbling Principle from docs/agent-guide.md: 5 quick toggles at top (1M context, thinking, effort, model, auto-updates), then categories with most-used expanded.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 128 vars listed from pkg/envvars.Registry()
- Quick toggles work (toggle writes/removes env block entry)
- Sensitive values masked, v reveals
- Source badges correct ([os-env], [env-block/user], etc.)

Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md

title: Hooks tab (Tab 3)
description:
Implement HooksModel: coverage map of 26 events, handler counts, blocking capability, expand to see individual handlers, distinguish Hitch-managed vs manual, add/disable/remove rules.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-05-settings-tui.md section 2 (Tab 3)
Reference: private/reference/tables-hooks.md
Existing: internal/state/ (RuleList, RuleDisable, RuleEnable, RuleRemove)

Acceptance criteria:
- All 26 events shown with handler counts
- Expand shows individual handlers with [ht:rule-id] or (manual) prefix
- a/d/e/r keys work for add/disable/enable/remove

Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md

title: Memory & Explorer tabs (Tabs 4-5)
description:
Implement MemoryModel (CLAUDE.md files, auto-memory, context estimate) and ExplorerModel (two-panel directory tree). These are read-only display tabs.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-05-settings-tui.md section 2 (Tabs 4-5)
Reference: private/reference/tables-storage-memory-context.md

Acceptance criteria:
- Memory tab shows all CLAUDE.md discovery paths
- Auto-memory progress bar (yellow >90%, red >97%)
- Context estimate total shown
- Explorer shows ~/.claude/ and ./.claude/ trees
- Enter opens file in $EDITOR

Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md

title: Wave 3A test quality review + docs + tryout
description:
Review TUI tests. Create internal/tui/README.md. Update CLAUDE.md. Create tryout task in hitch-r3u:
'Tryout: Wave 3A Settings TUI — run ht settings, navigate all 5 tabs, try editing a setting, toggling an env var, expanding hooks, checking memory. Report UX feedback.'

title: Alerting system
description:
Implement AlertEvaluator with 7 alert types, deduplication (10min cooldown per session), dispatch via internal/adapters.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-07-daemon-monitoring.md section 7
Existing: internal/adapters/ (Adapter.Send)

Acceptance criteria:
- burn_rate_spike fires when threshold exceeded
- rate_limit_hit fires on StopFailure
- Deduplication prevents repeat alerts within cooldown
- Alerts appear in GET /api/alerts

Testing standard: Read docs/testing.md

title: Tryout: Wave 1A settings and envvars
description:
Wave 1A (pkg/settings + pkg/envvars) is complete. Try:

1. go test ./pkg/settings/... -v
2. go test ./pkg/envvars/... -v
3. go test ./internal/generator/... -v (verify shim works)
4. go test ./... -count=1 (full suite)
5. ./ht sync --dry-run (verify sync still works after migration)
6. Check pkg/envvars has all vars: write a quick test or grep
7. Read: pkg/settings/README.md, pkg/envvars/README.md

Report findings, then create follow-up tasks if needed.

title: Tryout: Wave 0 shared packages
description:
Wave 0 shared infrastructure is complete. Try the following:

1. Build: go build -o ht ./cmd/ht
2. Run all tests: go test ./... -count=1
3. Check the new packages exist and compile:
   - go test ./internal/pricing/... -v
   - go test ./internal/tokens/... -v
   - go test ./internal/metrics/... -v
4. Verify proxy still works with extracted pricing:
   - ./ht proxy status (if proxy running)
   - ./ht proxy tail -n 5 (check cost calculations still work)
5. Read the new READMEs:
   - internal/pricing/README.md
   - internal/tokens/README.md
   - internal/metrics/README.md

Report: What works, what does not, any gaps or ideas for improvement. Comment on this task with findings, then create follow-up tasks if needed.

title: Human Tryout & Verification
description:
Human-gated epic for hands-on testing after each wave completes. Each task describes specific commands to run, things to try, and asks for feedback on what works, what doesn't, and what gaps exist. Tasks are created by the final task in each implementation epic.

This epic is the STOP GATE between waves. Work pauses here until the human has tested and provided feedback.

title: Tryout: Wave 1B hook engine
description:
Wave 1B hook engine update complete. Try:

1. go test ./... -count=1
2. ./ht rule add "on stop-failure -> log"
3. ./ht rule add "on user-prompt -> log"
4. ./ht rule add "on subagent-start -> deny \"no opus\" if model contains \"opus\""
5. ./ht sync --dry-run (verify new events generate valid settings.json entries)
6. ./ht rule list (should show all new rules)
7. Test parsing: echo various DSL strings through the parser

Report findings, gaps, ideas.

title: Wave 5A: SPEC-09 Security Audit
description:
Security audit process for third-party tools. First application: cozempic. Extract architecture and strategy documentation regardless of use/rebuild decision.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-09-security-audit.md
Cozempic source: ~/downloads/cozempic (cloned locally)
Output: ~/.hitch/audits/cozempic.md, documentation of all 18 pruning strategies

Independent — can start anytime. Blocks SPEC-10 cozempic integration.

title: Cozempic source code audit
description:
Perform the 8-section security audit checklist on cozempic. The source is cloned at ~/downloads/cozempic.

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-09-security-audit.md sections 2, 3
Source to audit: ~/downloads/cozempic/

For EACH of the 8 sections:
- Network Analysis: grep for urllib, requests, httpx, socket. Search for hardcoded URLs.
- Filesystem Access: grep for os.path, Path(, open(, os.environ. Check for .credentials.json access.
- Install Scope: inspect pyproject.toml/setup.py for post-install hooks. Trace cozempic init.
- Dependency Supply Chain: check for zero-dep claim, run pip install --dry-run if possible.
- Code Review Scope: count lines, count files, assess readability.
- Data Exfiltration Risk: cross-reference network + filesystem findings.
- Update Mechanism: find the 'daily PyPI monitoring' code. Can it be disabled?
- Runtime Behavior: grep for subprocess, os.system. Trace guard daemon behavior.

Resolve ALL UNKNOWN verdicts from the preliminary audit in section 3.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 8 sections have final verdict (PASS/CONDITIONAL/FAIL, no UNKNOWN)
- Write complete audit to ~/.hitch/audits/cozempic.md using template from spec section 5
- Decision recorded (A: Use As-Is, B: Fork and Pin, C: Extract and Rebuild, D: Skip)

title: Extract and document cozempic pruning strategies
description:
Regardless of the audit decision, document ALL 18 pruning strategies that cozempic implements. For each strategy: what it does, what JSONL structures it targets, expected byte savings, implementation approach in Go.

Source: ~/downloads/cozempic/ (read the actual strategy implementations)
Reference: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-10-integrations.md section 2.3 (Path B package layout)

Output: private/cozempic-strategies.md — a complete reference document that enables implementing any strategy in Go without referencing cozempic source again.

Group strategies by tier:
- Gentle: which strategies, what they remove, est. savings
- Standard: which strategies, what they remove, est. savings
- Aggressive: which strategies, what they remove, est. savings

Also document:
- The behavioral digest feature (what corrections are extracted, how)
- The guard daemon (what triggers it, what it reads, what it writes)
- The four-tier threshold logic (25/55/80/90%)

Acceptance criteria:
- private/cozempic-strategies.md exists with all 18 strategies documented
- Each strategy has: name, tier, what it removes, JSONL structure it targets, implementation notes
- Behavioral digest documented
- Guard daemon behavior documented

title: Create audit template
description:
Create ~/.hitch/audits/TEMPLATE.md using the format from SPEC-09 section 5. Also create a lightweight audit for ccusage at ~/.hitch/audits/ccusage.md (ccusage is low-risk: no network calls at runtime, reads only local files, well-maintained).

Spec: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-09-security-audit.md section 5

Acceptance criteria:
- ~/.hitch/audits/TEMPLATE.md exists
- ~/.hitch/audits/ccusage.md exists with preliminary audit

title: Create Wave 5A tryout task for human epic
description:
Create a beads task in hitch-r3u:

Title: 'Tryout: Wave 5A security audit results'
Assignee: human, Priority: 3
Description: 'Security audit and strategy extraction complete. Review:

1. Read ~/.hitch/audits/cozempic.md — the full audit
2. Read private/cozempic-strategies.md — all 18 pruning strategies documented
3. Decision recorded in audit: [A/B/C/D]

Your decision points:
- If Decision A (use as-is): proceed with SPEC-10 Path A hook wiring
- If Decision B (fork): fork repo, strip auto-update, pin version
- If Decision C (rebuild): proceed with SPEC-10 Path B Go rebuild
- If Decision D (skip): close cozempic integration tasks

Also review ~/.hitch/audits/ccusage.md for ccusage audit.

Comment with your decision and any feedback.'

func Estimate(charCount int) int — returns charCount/4 with rounding (if charCount%4 >= 2, round up)

- Unit tests verify: Estimate(0)==0, Estimate(1)==0, Estimate(2)==1, Estimate(4)==1, Estimate(5)==1, Estimate(6)==2, Estimate(100)==25

title: Create internal/metrics/ package

Create internal/metrics/ with canonical burn rate and token velocity definitions.

Files to create: internal/metrics/burnrate.go, internal/metrics/burnrate_test.go

Implementation:
- BurnRate(samples []BurnSample, windowDuration time.Duration) float64 — computes tokens/minute from a slice of timestamped cumulative token counts over a rolling window
- BurnSample struct: Timestamp time.Time, CumulativeTokens int
- Cache read tokens weighted at 1/10th for billing-accurate velocity (per SPEC-02 section 4.1)

Reference: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-02-hook-engine-update.md section 4.1 (burn_rate definition)
Reference: private/rev-2-specs/SPEC-07-daemon-monitoring.md section 4.4 (burn rate calculation)

4. Would a broken implementation pass these tests?

- Each test file reviewed with specific findings
- Any gaps identified with concrete suggestions for additional test cases

- go test ./... -count=1 passes

title: Create Wave 0 tryout task for human epic
description:

Title: 'Tryout: Wave 0 shared packages'
Type: task
Assignee: human
Priority: 3
Description: 'Wave 0 shared infrastructure is complete. Try the following:

1. Build: go build -o ht ./cmd/ht
2. Run all tests: go test ./... -count=1
3. Check the new packages exist and compile:
   - go test ./internal/pricing/... -v
   - go test ./internal/tokens/... -v
   - go test ./internal/metrics/... -v
4. Verify proxy still works with extracted pricing:
   - ./ht proxy status (if proxy running)
   - ./ht proxy tail -n 5 (check cost calculations still work)
5. Read the new READMEs:
   - internal/pricing/README.md
   - internal/tokens/README.md
   - internal/metrics/README.md

Report: What works, what does not, any gaps or ideas for improvement. Comment on this task with findings, then create follow-up tasks if needed.'

- Task description contains all tryout instructions above
